marketState: neutral - bearish storage overhang vs bullish demand momentum, near-term overbought
summary: Henry Hub front-month (Aug'26) at $3.245 trades $0.316 above the current fair-value estimate ($2.929, band $2.429-$3.429) and about 2.3pp above its seasonal median, a modest but not extreme premium. Weather demand is running +9% to +14% above the 5-year normal across both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows (support for price), though the 24h demand trend just fell sharply (-22.6) off a recent heat spike, hinting the current premium could fade near-term. Working against the bulls, storage sits +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +13 Bcf surprise build, and seasonality shows only a 19% historical chance of higher prices in 4 weeks with a typical -1.8% move - though that reliability is explicitly flagged Low (51pp spread) and September, historically the strongest month, falls inside the 3-12 week window. Managed money is unusually net short (18th 5y percentile), a contrarian bullish tell that limits further downside fuel from spec selling, while short-term technicals (5m RSI 68, price above upper Bollinger) look overbought against a neutral-to-bearish daily/weekly backdrop (50<200 EMA cross, bearish weekly trend). Net effect: fundamentals are too mixed and low-reliability for a confident multi-week swing call, but the near-term setup - rich vs fair value, overbought, decelerating heat - favors fading strength back toward fair value.

longTerm.rationale: Bullish demand (weather 8-16D +14% vs 5y, contrarian-bullish COT positioning at 18th percentile) is offset by bearish supply (storage +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish surprise build) and a seasonality signal explicitly flagged Low reliability; the setup doesn't clear the bar for a directional multi-week position yet.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained break of the fair-value band (below $2.43 or above $3.43) or a clear divergence of storage deviation vs 5y (narrowing sharply bullish, widening further bearish) would resolve the conflict.
longTerm.trigger: Long if price pulls back into the $2.95-$3.05 fair-value zone while weather demand stays above-normal into September (seasonally strongest month); short if storage surplus keeps widening and price fails to hold $3.10 with weekly bearish confirmation.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; scale half out at TP1, trail remainder to TP2
shortTerm.rationale: Front price is running $0.316 rich to the now-fair value of $2.929, 5m/15m technicals are overbought (RSI 68, price above upper Bollinger) with a negative daily EMA cross, and the 24h weather-demand trend just dropped sharply (-22.6) off its recent peak, all pointing to a near-term mean-reversion pullback toward fair value.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close back above $3.36 or a resumption of the 8-16D demand uptrend (rangePos pushing toward/above 0.85 with rising 24h change) would invalidate the short.
